name: vedro-pw

metadata.name: vedro-pw
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Integrates Playwright for automated browser testing  with customizable configuration options

documentation:
# vedro-pw [![Codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/vedro-universe/vedro-pw/main.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/vedro-universe/vedro-pw) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/vedro-pw.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-pw/) [![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/vedro-pw?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-pw/) [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/vedro-pw.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-pw/) The `vedro-pw` plugin allows you to use [Playwright](https://playwright.dev/) within your [Vedro](https://vedro.io/) scenarios for end-to-end testing of web applications. ## Installation
Quick

For a quick installation, you can use a plugin manager as follows: ```shell $ vedro plugin install vedro-pw ```

Manual

To install manually, follow these steps: 1. Install the package using pip: ```shell $ pip3 install vedro-pw ``` 2. Next, activate the plugin in your `vedro.cfg.py` configuration file: ```python # ./vedro.cfg.py import vedro import vedro_pw class Config(vedro.Config): class Plugins(vedro.Config.Plugins): class Playwright(vedro_pw.Playwright): enabled = True ```

## Usage Use the provided context functions in your scenarios to interact with Playwright: - `launched_browser`: Launches a local or remote browser based on the configuration. - `created_browser_context`: Creates a new browser context. - `opened_browser_page`: Opens a new page in the browser context. ### Basic Example Here's a simple Vedro scenario that opens the Playwright homepage and verifies the page title. ```python import vedro from vedro_pw import opened_browser_page class Scenario(vedro.Scenario): subject = "Open Playwright homepage" async def given(self): self.page = await opened_browser_page() async def when(self): await self.page.goto("https://playwright.dev/") async def then(self): assert await self.page.title() == "Playwright" ``` ## Command-Line Options The plugin adds several command-line arguments for flexibility: | Option | Description | Default | |------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------| | `--pw-browser` | Browser to use (`chromium`, `firefox`, `webkit`) | `chromium` | | `--pw-headed` | Run browser in headed mode | `False` | | `--pw-headless` | Run browser in headless mode | `True` | | `--pw-slowmo` | Delay operations by specified milliseconds | `0` | | `--pw-remote` | Connect to a remote browser instance | `False` | | `--pw-remote-endpoint` | WebSocket endpoint for remote browser | `ws://localhost:3000` | | `--pw-screenshots` | Screenshot capturing (`always`, `on-failure`, `on-reschedule`, `never`) | `never` | | `--pw-video` | Video recording (`always`, `on-failure`, `on-reschedule`, `never`) | `never` | | `--pw-trace` | Trace recording (`always`, `on-failure`, `on-reschedule`, `never`) | `never` | | `--pw-device` | Emulate a specific device | `None` | | `--pw-debug` | Enable Playwright debug mode by setting `PWDEBUG=1` | `False` | | `--pw-open-last-trace` | Open the last captured Playwright trace after the test run | `False` | ### Example Usage ```shell $ vedro run --pw-browser=firefox --pw-headed --pw-screenshots=on-failure --save-artifacts ``` ### Capture Modes `CaptureMode` determines when to capture artifacts: - `never`: Do not capture. - `on-failure`: Capture only when a scenario fails. - `on-reschedule`: Capture when a scenario is rescheduled. - `always`: Always capture. Artifacts like screenshots, videos, and traces are attached to the scenario results and can be used in reports.

modules.name: vedro_pw._unpack
documentation:
Provides compatibility for the `Unpack` type hint across Python versions.

This module ensures that the `Unpack` type hint is available regardless of whether it is natively
available in the `typing` module (for newer Python versions) or needs to be imported from
`typing_extensions`. If neither is available, it defines a fallback for `Unpack`.

classes.name: vedro_pw._pw_browser.PlaywrightBrowser

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_browser.PlaywrightBrowser.__str__
documentation:
Return the string representation of the PlaywrightBrowser.

```
return

The string value of the browser type.
```

Defines the supported browser types for Playwright.

This enumeration includes the following browser types:
- `FIREFOX`: Use the Mozilla Firefox browser.
- `CHROMIUM`: Use the Chromium browser.
- `WEBKIT`: Use the WebKit browser.

Additionally, it supports:
- `RANDOM`: Randomly select a browser type.

These values can be used to specify the browser to be launched or connected to.

classes.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig

methods.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.add_browser_context
documentation:
Add a browser context to the tracked list of active contexts.

```
param context

The browser context to add.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.remove_browser_context
documentation:
Remove a browser context from the tracked list of active contexts.

```
param context

The browser context to remove.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.get_browser_contexts
documentation:
Retrieve the list of active browser contexts.

```
return

A list of active browser contexts.
```

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.browser_name
documentation: The browser to use for sessions. Can be `CHROMIUM`, `FIREFOX`, or `WEBKIT`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.device_name
documentation: The device to emulate during browser sessions, or `None` to disable device emulation.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.headed
documentation: Whether to launch the browser in headed (visible UI) mode.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.slowmo
documentation: Delay (in milliseconds) between Playwright operations.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.remote
documentation: Indicates if a remote browser instance should be used.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.remote_endpoint
documentation: WebSocket URL for connecting to a remote browser instance, if `remote` is `True`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.timeout
documentation:
Default timeout (in milliseconds) for browser operations, such as page load or
element interactions.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.navigation_timeout
documentation: Timeout (in milliseconds) for browser navigation operations, such as page.goto().

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.browser_timeout
documentation: Timeout (in milliseconds) for launching the browser.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.browser_contexts
documentation: Tracks active browser contexts as a dictionary where the keys are context instances.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.should_capture_trace
documentation: Indicates whether to enable trace recording during browser sessions.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.trace_options
documentation: Configuration options for trace recording, including file paths, names, and captured data.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.should_capture_video
documentation: Indicates whether video recording should be enabled for browser sessions.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.video_options
documentation: Configuration options for video recording, such as file formats and storage paths.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.should_capture_screenshots
documentation: Indicates whether screenshots should be captured during browser sessions.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw._runtime_config.RuntimeConfig.screenshot_options
documentation: Configuration options for screenshot capturing, including format, quality, and storage paths.

Holds runtime configurations for Playwright browser sessions.

This configuration class manages various browser-related settings such as
the browser type, device emulation, timeouts, and artifact capture options
(e.g., traces, videos, screenshots). It also tracks active browser contexts.

classes.name: vedro_pw._capture_mode.CaptureMode

methods.name: vedro_pw._capture_mode.CaptureMode.__str__
documentation:
Return the string representation of the CaptureMode.

```
return

The string value of the CaptureMode.
```

Defines modes for controlling the behavior of capturing screenshots, video, or traces.

This enumeration includes the following modes:
- `ALWAYS`: Always capture the artifact.
- `NEVER`: Never capture the artifact.
- `ON_RESCHEDULE`: Capture the artifact only when the scenario is rescheduled.
- `ON_FAILURE`: Capture the artifact only when the scenario or step fails.

classes.name: vedro_pw._configurable_browser.ConfigurableBrowser

methods.name: vedro_pw._configurable_browser.ConfigurableBrowser.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the ConfigurableBrowser.

```
param impl_obj

The underlying Playwright  instance.

param device_options

Optional device-specific options for emulation.

param runtime_config

The runtime configuration for the browser.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._configurable_browser.ConfigurableBrowser.new_context
documentation:
Create a new browser context with integrated runtime configurations.

This method applies device options, runtime settings, and captures
artifacts such as videos or traces if configured.

```
param kwargs

Additional options to override or extend the context settings.

return

A new  instance with the configured options.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._configurable_browser.ConfigurableBrowser.new_page
documentation:
Create a new browser page within a newly created context.

This method creates a new context and opens a single page within it.

```
param kwargs

Additional options to override or extend the context settings.

return

A new  instance within the created context.
```

Extends the Playwright `Browser` to provide configurable options and runtime integration.

This class integrates device options and runtime configurations, such as timeouts,
video recording, and tracing, into the creation of browser contexts and pages.

functions.name: vedro_pw._utils.get_browser_type
documentation:
Retrieve the `BrowserType` instance corresponding to the given browser name.

If `PlaywrightBrowser.RANDOM` is passed, a random browser type is selected
from the available options (`CHROMIUM`, `FIREFOX`, `WEBKIT`).

```
param playwright

The Playwright instance to retrieve the browser type from.

param browser_name

The name of the browser, either as a  enum
or a string.

return

The  instance for the specified browser.

raises AttributeError

If the specified browser is not available in the Playwright instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw._utils.get_device_options
documentation:
Retrieve the device options for the specified device name.

Device options define emulation settings such as viewport size, user agent,
and other device-specific properties. This function ensures the device exists
in Playwright's supported devices.

```
param playwright

The Playwright instance to retrieve device options from.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate. If None, no device
options are returned.

return

A  dictionary containing the emulation options, or None
if  is None.

raises ValueError

If the specified device is not supported by Playwright.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw._utils.show_pw_trace
documentation:
Open the Playwright trace viewer for the specified trace file.

This function launches a subprocess to open the Playwright trace viewer
for analyzing the specified trace file. The viewer runs as a separate
process, and this function does not block execution.

```
param path

The file path to the trace file to be opened.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.launched_browser
documentation:
Launch a local browser instance.

```
param browser_name

The name of the browser to launch.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate.

param auto_close

Whether to close the browser automatically when the scenario ends.

param playwright

An optional Playwright instance to use.

param kwargs

Additional launch options for the browser.

return

A ConfigurableBrowser instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.launched_browser
documentation:
Connect to a remote browser instance.

```
param browser_name

The name of the browser to connect to.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate.

param auto_close

Whether to close the browser automatically when the scenario ends.

param playwright

An optional Playwright instance to use.

param kwargs

Additional connection options for the browser.

return

A ConfigurableBrowser instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.launched_browser
documentation:
Launch or connect to a browser instance based on runtime configuration.

```
param browser_name

The name of the browser to launch or connect to.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate.

param auto_close

Whether to close the browser automatically when the scenario ends.

param playwright

An optional Playwright instance to use.

param kwargs

Additional options for the browser.

return

A ConfigurableBrowser instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.launched_local_browser
documentation:
Launch a local browser instance.

```
param browser_name

The name of the browser to launch.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate.

param auto_close

Whether to close the browser automatically when the scenario ends.

param playwright

An optional Playwright instance to use.

param kwargs

Additional launch options for the browser.

return

A ConfigurableBrowser instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.launched_remote_browser
documentation:
Connect to a remote browser instance.

```
param browser_name

The name of the browser to connect to.

param device_name

The name of the device to emulate.

param auto_close

Whether to close the browser automatically when the scenario ends.

param playwright

An optional Playwright instance to use.

param kwargs

Additional connection options for the browser.

return

A ConfigurableBrowser instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.created_browser_context
documentation:
Create a new browser context.

```
param browser

An optional Browser instance. If None, a browser is launched.

param kwargs

Additional options for creating the browser context.

return

A BrowserContext instance.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api.opened_browser_page
documentation:
Open a new page in the given browser context.

```
param context

An optional BrowserContext instance. If None, a context is created.

return

A Page instance representing the new browser page.
```

functions.name: vedro_pw.async_api._get_playwright_instance
documentation:
Get a Playwright instance.

```
param auto_close

Whether to automatically close the Playwright instance when
the scenario ends.

return

An AsyncPlaywright instance.
```

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.executable_path
documentation: Path to a browser executable to run instead of the bundled one.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.channel
documentation: Browser distribution channel. Supported values include 'chrome', 'msedge', etc.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.args
documentation: Additional arguments to pass to the browser instance.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.ignore_default_args
documentation: If `True`, disables Playwright's default browser arguments.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.handle_sigint
documentation: Close the browser process on Ctrl-C.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.handle_sigterm
documentation: Close the browser process on SIGTERM.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.handle_sighup
documentation: Close the browser process on SIGHUP.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.timeout
documentation: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the browser instance to start.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.env
documentation: Environment variables to be visible to the browser.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.headless
documentation: Run the browser in headless mode.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.devtools
documentation: Auto-open a Developer Tools panel for each tab (Chromium-only).

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.proxy
documentation: Network proxy settings.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.downloads_path
documentation: Path where accepted downloads are stored.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.slow_mo
documentation: Slows down Playwright operations by the specified milliseconds.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.traces_dir
documentation: Path to save browser traces.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.chromium_sandbox
documentation: Enable or disable Chromium sandboxing.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.LaunchOptions.firefox_user_prefs
documentation: Custom Firefox user preferences.

Represents the options for launching a browser instance.

Defines all configurable parameters for the `launch` method of the `BrowserType` class.

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions.ws_endpoint
documentation: A browser websocket endpoint to connect to.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions.timeout
documentation: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the connection to be established.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions.slow_mo
documentation: Slows down Playwright operations by the specified amount of milliseconds.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions.headers
documentation: Additional HTTP headers to be sent with the web socket connect request.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ConnectOptions.expose_network
documentation: Defines network rules to expose from the client to the connected browser.

Represents the options for connecting to an existing browser instance.

Defines all configurable parameters for the `connect` method of the `BrowserType` class.

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.viewport
documentation: Sets a consistent viewport for each page. Defaults to 1280x720 if not disabled by `no_viewport`

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.screen
documentation: Emulates consistent window screen size available inside web pages.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.no_viewport
documentation: Disables fixed viewport, allowing window resizing in headed mode.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.ignore_https_errors
documentation: Specifies whether to ignore HTTPS errors when sending network requests.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.java_script_enabled
documentation: Specifies whether JavaScript should be enabled. Defaults to `true`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.bypass_csp
documentation: Enables bypassing of page's Content-Security-Policy.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.user_agent
documentation: Specifies the user agent string to use in this context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.locale
documentation: Specifies the user locale, affecting `navigator.language` and date/number formatting.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.timezone_id
documentation: Specifies the timezone for the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.geolocation
documentation: Sets geolocation for the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.permissions
documentation: List of permissions to grant to all pages in the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.extra_http_headers
documentation: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request in the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.offline
documentation: Emulates offline network conditions.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.http_credentials
documentation: Credentials for HTTP authentication.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.device_scale_factor
documentation: Specifies the device scale factor (similar to DPR).

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.is_mobile
documentation: Specifies if the context should emulate a mobile device.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.has_touch
documentation: Specifies whether the viewport should support touch events.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.color_scheme
documentation: Emulates the `prefers-color-scheme` media feature.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.reduced_motion
documentation: Emulates the `prefers-reduced-motion` media feature.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.forced_colors
documentation: Emulates the `forced-colors` media feature.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.accept_downloads
documentation: Automatically accepts all downloads in the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.default_browser_type
documentation: Specifies the default browser type for the context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.proxy
documentation: Configures network proxy settings.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_har_path
documentation: Specifies the file path to save HAR recordings.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_har_omit_content
documentation: Controls whether to omit request content from HAR recordings.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_video_dir
documentation: Specifies the directory to save recorded videos.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_video_size
documentation: Specifies the dimensions of recorded videos.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.storage_state
documentation: Populates the context with given storage state (e.g., cookies and localStorage).

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.base_url
documentation: Specifies the base URL for relative navigations.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.strict_selectors
documentation: Enables strict selectors mode for this context.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.service_workers
documentation: Controls whether service workers can be registered.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_har_url_filter
documentation: Specifies a URL filter for HAR recordings.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_har_mode
documentation: Controls the level of detail recorded in HAR files.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.record_har_content
documentation: Specifies how to handle resource content in HAR files.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.NewContextOptions.client_certificates
documentation: Configures TLS client certificates for the context.

Represents the options for creating a new browser context.

Defines all configurable parameters for the `new_context` method of the `Browser` class.

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.viewport
documentation:
The viewport dimensions of the device. Specifies the width and height
for rendering web pages.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.user_agent
documentation:
The user agent string to use for emulation. This string identifies
the browser and device type during requests.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.is_mobile
documentation:
Whether to emulate mobile device behavior, including considering
the meta viewport tag. Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.has_touch
documentation: Indicates if the device supports touch input. Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.device_scale_factor
documentation:
The device scale factor (DPR - Device Pixel Ratio). This controls the
density of pixels emulated for the device. Defaults to `1.0`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.DeviceOptions.default_browser_type
documentation:
The default browser type to use in the context. Can be `chromium`,
`firefox`, or `webkit`.

Represents device options for configuring browser contexts.

This TypedDict provides a structured way to define emulation options
such as viewport, user agent, and device-specific characteristics
(e.g., mobile settings and touch support).

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.path
documentation: The file path where the final trace zip file will be saved. Used in `tracing.stop()`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.name
documentation:
The prefix for intermediate trace files, stored in the `tracesDir` directory
specified in `browser_type.launch()`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.title
documentation: The title of the trace to be displayed in the Trace Viewer.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.screenshots
documentation:
Indicates whether to capture screenshots during tracing to build a timeline preview.
Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.snapshots
documentation:
Indicates whether to capture DOM snapshots and record network activity during tracing.
Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.TraceOptions.sources
documentation:
Indicates whether to include source files for trace actions in the trace output.
Defaults to `False`.

Represents the configuration options for saving a trace during tracing.

This TypedDict defines optional parameters to customize trace capturing and saving,
such as file paths, trace names, and included data (e.g., snapshots, screenshots).

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.VideoOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.VideoOptions.record_video_dir
documentation:
The directory where recorded videos for all pages in the browser context will be saved.
If not specified, videos will not be recorded. Make sure to call
`browser_context.close()` to ensure that videos are saved properly.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.VideoOptions.record_video_size
documentation:
The dimensions of the recorded videos, defined by width and height.

- If not specified, the size defaults to the `viewport` dimensions scaled down to fit 800x800.
- If `viewport` is not explicitly configured, the video size defaults to 800x450.
- Each page's video will be scaled down as necessary to fit the specified size.

Represents the configuration options for video recording in a browser context.

This TypedDict defines optional parameters to enable and customize video
recording, such as the output directory and the dimensions of the recorded videos.

classes.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.timeout
documentation:
Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the screenshot to be taken.
Defaults to `30000` (30 seconds). Pass `0` to disable the timeout.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.type
documentation:
The image format for the screenshot. Can be either `"jpeg"` or `"png"`.
Defaults to `"png"`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.path
documentation:
The file path where the screenshot will be saved. The image format is
inferred from the file extension. If no path is specified, the screenshot
is not saved to disk.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.quality
documentation:
The quality of the image, specified as an integer between 0 and 100.
This is only applicable to `"jpeg"` images. Ignored for `"png"` images.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.omit_background
documentation:
Whether to hide the default white background and capture the screenshot
with transparency. This option is not applicable to `"jpeg"` images.
Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.full_page
documentation:
Whether to capture a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead
of just the visible viewport. Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.clip
documentation:
Specifies a rectangular region of the page to clip for the screenshot.
The rectangle is defined by its x and y coordinates, width, and height.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.animations
documentation:
Specifies how animations are handled during the screenshot:

- `"allow"`: Leaves animations untouched (default).
- `"disabled"`: Disables CSS animations, CSS transitions, and Web Animations.
    - Finite animations are fast-forwarded to completion (fires `transitionend`).
    - Infinite animations are canceled to their initial state.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.caret
documentation:
Controls the visibility of the text caret during the screenshot:

- `"hide"`: Hides the text caret (default).
- `"initial"`: Leaves the text caret unchanged.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.scale
documentation:
Determines the scaling of the screenshot:

- `"css"`: Captures one pixel per CSS pixel on the page (default for high-dpi).
- `"device"`: Captures one pixel per device pixel, producing larger screenshots
  on high-dpi devices.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.mask
documentation:
A list of locators specifying elements to mask in the screenshot. Masked
elements are overlaid with a box of the color specified by `mask_color`.

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.mask_color
documentation:
The color of the overlay box used for masking elements, specified in CSS
color format (e.g., `#FF00FF` for pink). The default color is pink (`#FF00FF`).

class_variables.name: vedro_pw.options.ScreenshotOptions.style
documentation:
A custom stylesheet to apply during the screenshot. This can be used to
hide dynamic elements, make elements invisible, or modify their properties
for creating repeatable screenshots. The stylesheet pierces the Shadow DOM
and applies to inner frames as well.

Represents the configuration options for taking a screenshot of a page.

This TypedDict defines optional parameters to control the screenshot
capture process, such as file path, format, quality, and additional
visual settings like animations, masking, and styles.

classes.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the PlaywrightPlugin with the provided configuration.

```
param config

The Playwright configuration class.

param runtime_config

The runtime configuration for Playwright.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribe to the necessary Vedro events for managing Playwright configurations.

```
param dispatcher

The event dispatcher to register listeners on.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_arg_parse
documentation:
Handle the event when command-line arguments are being parsed.

Adds Playwright-specific arguments to the argument parser.

```
param event

The ArgParseEvent instance containing the argument parser.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_arg_parsed
documentation:
Handle the event after command-line arguments have been parsed.

Sets runtime configuration values based on the parsed arguments.

```
param event

The ArgParsedEvent instance containing parsed arguments.

raises ValueError

If the  value is negative.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_scenario_run
documentation:
Handle the event when a scenario begins execution.

Configures trace, video, and screenshot capturing based on the scenario's state.

```
param event

The ScenarioRunEvent instance.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._should_capture
documentation:
Determine if an artifact should be captured based on the capture mode.

```
param capture_mode

The mode specifying when to capture the artifact.

param is_rescheduled

Whether the scenario has been rescheduled.

return

True if the artifact should be captured, False otherwise.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._should_retain
documentation:
Determine if a captured artifact should be retained based on the capture mode.

```
param capture_mode

The mode specifying when to retain the artifact.

param is_failed

Whether the scenario or step has failed.

return

True if the artifact should be retained, False otherwise.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_step_end
documentation:
Handle the event when a step completes execution.

Captures screenshots of the current state of the browser, if configured.

```
param event

The StepPassedEvent or StepFailedEvent instance.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._capture_screenshot
documentation:
Capture a screenshot of the current browser page and save it to a temporary file.

This method generates a unique file name based on the step number and step name,
saves the screenshot to a temporary file, and returns the file path.

```
param page

The Playwright  instance to capture the screenshot from.

param step_name

The name of the step, used in the screenshot file name.

return

The path to the temporary file where the screenshot is saved.

raises playwright.async_api.Error

If the screenshot capture fails.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_scenario_end
documentation:
Handle the event when a scenario completes execution.

Attaches any captured artifacts (trace, video, screenshots) to the scenario result.

```
param event

The ScenarioPassedEvent or ScenarioFailedEvent instance.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._find_file
documentation:
Find the first file in the given directory.

```
param directory

The directory to search for files.

return

The Path to the first file, or None if no files are found.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._create_trace_artifact
documentation:
Create a FileArtifact for a captured trace.

```
param trace_path

The Path to the trace file.

return

A FileArtifact representing the trace.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._create_video_artifact
documentation:
Create a FileArtifact for a captured video.

```
param video_path

The Path to the video file.

return

A FileArtifact representing the video.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin._create_screenshot_artifact
documentation:
Create a FileArtifact for a captured screenshot.

```
param screenshot_path

The Path to the screenshot file.

return

A FileArtifact representing the screenshot.
```

methods.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.PlaywrightPlugin.on_cleanup
documentation:
Handle the cleanup event to finalize the Playwright test session.

If the `--pw-open-last-trace` option is enabled and a trace was captured,
this method attempts to open the Playwright trace viewer for the last captured trace.

```
param event

The CleanupEvent instance triggered during cleanup.
```

Integrates Playwright with Vedro for browser-based testing.

This plugin provides configurations for browser type, screenshot capturing,
video recording, trace capturing, and more. It manages the Playwright runtime
configurations and collects artifacts during test execution.

classes.name: vedro_pw._pw_plugin.Playwright
documentation:
Configuration class for the PlaywrightPlugin.

Provides default settings for browser automation, including browser type,
device emulation, trace capturing, video recording, and screenshot capturing.
